campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund scholarships for local youth recreation programs. The council also discussed extending a temporary local rule (Ordinance 2326) and advanced plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of Ordinance 2326 (temporary local rule)
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue overcrossing study

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new city rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council decided to move forward with the proposal by skipping the usual first step and planning to officially approve it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for resident input on a new ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to speed up approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next council meeting

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize upcoming agendas for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, set specific times for certain topics to be discussed, and checked that all required public notices were properly filed.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 meeting agenda
Planned items for the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Set time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewed public notice requirements and waivers
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed committee reports and scheduled upcoming board and commission meetings, with agendas posted at least 24 hours in advance.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Agendas available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and approve funding applications for community development projects aimed at improving housing and services in Sunnyvale. The meeting included approval of previous meeting minutes and presentations from organizations seeking Community Development Block Grant money to support local housing and human services programs.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications being reviewed
Housing and human services projects seeking city support
Approval of February 25, 2026 meeting minutes
Presentations from organizations requesting development funding

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching a amnesty program for business licenses, reaching out to shopping centers, and promoting local shopping through summer campaigns. They discussed ways to partner with private companies to sponsor city events and offer incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get compliant without penalties
Shopping center outreach to boost participation in city initiatives
Summer Shop Local campaigns to encourage residents to buy from Cupertino businesses
Public-private partnerships to fund city events and reduce city budget strain
Tax breaks and incentives for new retail stores and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed properties or projects related to the city's historical sites and landmarks. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission by email deadline (12 p.m. day of meeting)
Review of historical properties or landmark designations

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative tasks and approve upcoming plans. The council reviewed their meeting schedule, approved previous meeting notes, and authorized Mayor Montano to attend a 2026 wellness conference. They also confirmed a representative to serve on the Economic Development board.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes and confirmed upcoming council calendar
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
Confirmed new Milpitas Unified School District representative to Economic Development committee
Reviewed upcoming agenda items requested by councilmembers

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to approve several items including a proclamation celebrating Women's History Month. The council also agreed to let the American Red Cross use city facilities during emergencies and approved a three-year contract to buy email security software (Mimecast) for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
